key_insight: VeriGeo generates geometry problems by coupling problem creation, diagram generation, and proof checking in one verifiable pipeline, making synthetic geometry data much more reliable.
engineering_relevance: The framework shows how executable reasoning traces and staged verification can improve controllable data synthesis for multimodal math systems and downstream solver training.
method: Author agent creates a problem, diagram, and shared action sequence from user constraints.
Solver agent produces a proof-aligned solution using the same verifiable representation.
Three-stage verification checks numerical consistency, analytical realizability, and global consistency, with reflection-based repair for recoverable errors.
evidence_signal: Across five LLM backbones, many raw generations fail verification, while VeriGeo repairs a substantial fraction; training on 8.7k VeriGeo-generated examples yields the best reported GeoQA performance and strong results on PGPS9K and MathVista-GPS.
watch_item: The pipeline depends on successful verification and repair, so unrecoverable inconsistencies or diagram/constraint mismatches may still limit coverage and add generation cost.
paper_title: VeriGeo: Controllable Geometry Question Generation with Numerical and Analytical Verification
